name: appstream
title: appstream
description:
Software component metadata management
AppStream is a metadata specification which permits software components to
provide information about themselves to automated systems and end-users
before the software is actually installed.
The AppStream project provides facilities to easily access and transform
this metadata, as well as a few additional services to allow building
feature-rich software centers and similar applications.
.
This package provides tools to generate, maintain and query the AppStream
data pool of installed and available software, and enables integration
with the APT package manager.
.
The 'appstreamcli' tool can be used for accessing the software component
pool as well as for working with AppStream metadata directly, including
validating it for compliance with the specification.

commands.name: appstreamcli
commands.description: Handle AppStream metadata formats and query AppStream data

arguments.description: Print out more information about a found component.
arguments.raw: --details  Print out more information about a found component.

arguments.description: Do not access the network when validating metadata.
arguments.raw: --no-net  Do not access the network when validating metadata.

arguments.description: Display the version number of appstreamcli
arguments.raw: --version  Display the version number of appstreamcli

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use appstreamcli because Software component metadata management.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use appstreamcli as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `appstreamcli [COMMAND]` as documented upstream for appstreamcli.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair appstreamcli with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `appstreamcli --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
